{'reasoning': "The goal is to generate a SPIR-V binary for a fragment shader that implements Blinn-Phong shading. The shader will calculate the output color based on normal, light direction, view direction, and a configurable shininess exponent (hardcoded to 64 for specular exponent). It takes world position, normal, UV, tangent, and color as inputs from the vertex shader. A Uniform Buffer Object (UBO) provides model, view, projection matrices, light position, camera position, and other parameters. The output color should be a grayscale intensity with alpha 1.0. The Blinn-Phong model is used, with diffuse component as max(dot(N,L), 0) and specular component as pow(max(dot(N,H), 0), 64), where H is the halfway vector. The final color is calculated as 0.1 + 0.6*diffuse + 0.8*specular. I will construct the SPIR-V binary by defining capabilities, extensions, memory model, entry point, execution modes, and then defining all necessary types (void, function, float, vector, matrix, pointer, sampler, etc.). I'll declare inputs and UBO, then define the main function. Inside the function, I will load necessary data from inputs and UBO, compute the light vector (L), view vector (V), normalize N, compute diffuse and specular terms, construct the output color, and return it. Specific SPIR-V opcodes like OpExtInst with GLSL.std.450 functions (normalize, dot, pow, max, add, mul, sub, fnegate, length, faceforward, reflect, inverse, transpose, etc.) will be used extensively. The UBO will be set up with appropriate decorations for binding and descriptor set. The final color will be a `vec4` with RGB components representing grayscale intensity and alpha set to 1.0. The bound ID will be carefully calculated to accommodate all used IDs.", 'spirv_hex': ''}
